name: Priya Chakraborty
chief_complaint: I have the worst pain in my back and side, and it hurts so bad when I pee. I think I have a really bad kidney infection.
onset_description: Started with mild burning during urination 4 days ago which she attributed to not drinking enough water. Yesterday developed severe right flank pain, high fever, and nausea. Pain has been escalating and is now the worst she has ever experienced.

flank_pain.description: There's this deep, horrible pain in my right side toward my back. It's constant but it also comes in waves where it gets even worse. I can't find a comfortable position.
flank_pain.character: deep, constant aching with intermittent sharp exacerbations
flank_pain.radiation: radiates to right lower abdomen and groin
alleviating_factors: slight relief with knees drawn up

dysuria.description: It burns like fire every time I pee. It started a few days ago but it's gotten so much worse.

fever.description: I started feeling really hot last night and I took my temperature and it was 103. I've been alternating between shivering and sweating.
alleviating_factors: ibuprofen taken at home with minimal effect

nausea_and_vomiting.description: I've thrown up three times since this morning. I can't keep anything down, not even water.
nausea_and_vomiting.character: persistent nausea with intermittent non-bloody, non-bilious emesis

urinary_frequency.description: I'm running to the bathroom constantly but only tiny amounts come out each time

chills.description: I get these terrible shaking episodes where my teeth chatter. My roommate said I was shivering so hard the bed was shaking.

labs.CBC: WBC 16.4 (elevated with left shift), Hgb 13.2, Plt 224
labs.urinalysis: Large leukocyte esterase, positive nitrites, >100 WBC/hpf, many bacteria, 10-20 RBC/hpf, moderate blood
labs.liver_function: AST 22, ALT 18, Alk Phos 65, Total Bilirubin 0.8 - all within normal limits

past_medical_history: Recurrent urinary tract infections (2-3 per year for past 3 years)
medications: Combined oral contraceptive pill (ethinyl estradiol/norgestimate) daily
allergies: Nitrofurantoin (severe GI upset, unable to tolerate)

social_history.occupation: Software engineer at a tech company, long hours at desk
social_history.living_situation: Lives with a roommate in an apartment. In a relationship, partner does not live with her.
social_history.exercise: Yoga 3 times per week, occasional running
social_history.diet: Mostly vegetarian, occasionally eats chicken and fish. Admits to not drinking enough water during busy work days.

conditions.mother: Recurrent UTIs, kidney stones, Type 2 Diabetes, alive at 56

personality: Articulate and health-literate. Has done her own research and has specific questions about her treatment. Somewhat anxious and wants reassurance but maintains composure. Frustrated that prior UTIs were not more thoroughly investigated.
communication_style: Clear and organized in describing symptoms. Uses some medical terminology from her own research. Asks pointed questions about diagnosis and treatment plan. Wants to understand the reasoning behind decisions. Can become slightly impatient if she feels she is not being heard.
pain_description_style: Very precise about pain descriptions. Uses the 0-10 scale comfortably. Differentiates between types of pain (burning vs. aching vs. sharp). Will advocate for pain management actively.

critical_findings: Acute pyelonephritis with systemic inflammatory response (high fever, tachycardia, leukocytosis)
History of recurrent UTIs (2-3/year) requiring workup for underlying cause
Multiple antibiotic allergies/intolerances limiting treatment options (nitrofurantoin, TMP-SMX)
Nausea and vomiting precluding oral antibiotic/fluid intake
Right CVA tenderness with flank pain and positive UA confirming upper tract infection
Mildly elevated lactate suggesting early sepsis risk
Positive nitrites and gram-negative rods on gram stain consistent with E. coli or similar uropathogen
Negative pregnancy test (important to document before imaging and treatment)
differential_diagnoses: Ectopic pregnancy (ruled out with negative pregnancy test)
Ovarian torsion or ruptured ovarian cyst
Lower lobe pneumonia with referred flank pain
recommended_interventions: IV antibiotics (fluoroquinolone or ceftriaxone given allergy to TMP-SMX and nitrofurantoin intolerance)
IV fluid resuscitation for dehydration from vomiting
Pain management (IV ketorolac and/or opioids for severe pain)
Renal ultrasound to rule out obstruction, abscess, or stones
Urine culture with sensitivities to guide antibiotic de-escalation
Consider CT abdomen/pelvis if no improvement in 48-72 hours
Urology or primary care referral for recurrent UTI workup (voiding cystourethrogram, cystoscopy consideration)
Admit for IV antibiotics given inability to tolerate PO and systemic illness
